text: We report the use of the molecular copper boron-imidazolate cage catalyst BIF-29(Cu) for the electroco-reduction of CO2 and NO3– into urea. We observe an exceptionally high selectivity of 68.5% at a current density of 424 μA cm–2 with BIF-29(Cu) being ranked amongst the most selective catalystes to ever be reported for this reaction. Remarkably, BIF-29(Cu) contains isolated, single-metal active sites. By combining electrochemical measurements with in situ mass spectrometry and ex-situ spectroscopies along with atomic-scale simulations, we elucidate the reaction mechanism. Our data reveals the parallel operation of the CO2 and NO3– reduction reactions on the copper sites of BIF-29(Cu) with the C–N bond formation occurring by the condensation of *CO with *NH2OH. Our results therefore demonstrate that supramolecular metal–organic cages, where specific discrete active sites can be designed, can facilitate multi-reactant complex coupling reactions with high efficiencies.
